literal: a person who cackles loudly or laughably; a habitual loud laugh or gossip
a person who plunges into activities or subjects energetically; one who 'dives' into matters without hesitation
a phrase denoting death or that which is dead
a romantic relationship that begins or takes place in the autumnal season
existing or occurring before the term or label for it was coined
a typical or ordinary person; a member of the general populace
an ordinary, typical person, especially an ordinary man
to raise one's average performance or measured value by earning higher subsequent results
mentally absent or preoccupied; behaving as if lost in thought or daydreaming
a proverbial observation that political circumstances and public opinion can change very quickly
a proverb expressing the idea that offspring reflect the nature of their parents
a concise acknowledgement indicating that an order has been heard, understood, and will be carried out
a person who is naive, inexperienced, or unsophisticated and therefore vulnerable to deception or danger
a person, object, or attribute that attracts a large number of people considered desirable as romantic or sexual partners
an expression describing a look of innocence, vulnerability, or childlike wonder conveyed through a person's eyes
the combination of desirable or valuable elements with undesirable ones
a conspicuous or uncomfortable problem or issue that is being ignored or avoided by a group
an expression used to reciprocate a greeting, compliment, insult, or good wish
a state of lower priority in which a task, plan, or issue is deliberately deferred for the time being
a figurative designation for tasks, projects, or concerns placed at low priority and deferred until a later time
